goal: Pull recent interaction logs and analyze how the hormonal channels (cortisol, serotonin, oxytocin) track across multiple turns in sequence to determine if positive interactions build and persist fast enough to buffer subsequent stressful moments.

plan_steps.description: Fetch recent interaction logs from the cloud storage.
acceptance_criteria: Recent interaction logs are successfully downloaded and saved locally.

plan_steps.description: Parse the interaction logs to extract cortisol, serotonin, and oxytocin levels for each turn.
acceptance_criteria: A structured dataset containing cortisol, serotonin, and oxytocin levels for each turn is created.

plan_steps.description: Analyze the extracted data to track how hormonal channels change across multiple turns.
acceptance_criteria: A report detailing the changes in cortisol, serotonin, and oxytocin levels over time is generated.

plan_steps.description: Determine if positive interactions build and persist fast enough to buffer subsequent stressful moments.
acceptance_criteria: A conclusion on whether positive interactions effectively buffer stress is provided.

spoken_summary: I don't have access to any interaction logs or hormonal data to analyze — those systems aren't available to me. I can only work with files and tools on your computer, so I'd need you to point me to where that data is stored if you want me to pull and analyze it.
